channel.title: Surviving the Singularity — Research Signals
channel.description: Algorithmically swept arXiv research ranked by singularity relevance. Not human-curated.

item.title: Can Agentic Trading Systems Pay for Their Own Intelligence?
item.description: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly used in trading systems, where model reasoning, tool use, and continual decisions incur costs that are expected to produce trading value. Existing evaluations typically report performance metrics, but rarely examine agentic viability: whether dynami... [Score: 9.5 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, tool use, reasoning, llm] Authors: Qiqi Duan, Changlun Li, et al.

item.title: A hierarchical memory architecture overcomes context limits in long-horizon multi-agent computational modeling
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) demonstrate remarkable reasoning capabilities, yet their stateless architecture fundamentally limits deployment in long-horizon research workflows requiring multi-session continuity and quantitative rigor. Here we present Ensemble QSP, a multi-agent framework featuring a... [Score: 9.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, autonomous, reasoning, llm] Authors: Shivendra G. Tewari, Holly Kimko

item.title: Agentic evolution of physically constrained foundation models
item.description: Artificial intelligence increasingly drives automated scientific discovery, yet contemporary generalist agents lack physical grounding, frequently hallucinating hardware-incompatible designs. Here, we present a physically grounded, multi-agent discovery engine that autonomously architects hardware-c... [Score: 9.5 | Flagged for: foundation model, agent, multi-agent, agentic, autonomous] Authors: Jiangwei Zhang, Wen Sun, et al.

item.title: OpenRCA 2.0: From Outcome Labels to Causal Process Supervision
item.description: Root cause analysis (RCA) poses a holistic test of LLM agentic capabilities, such as long-context understanding, multi-step reasoning, and tool use. However, existing datasets suffer from a fundamental gap: they label only the root cause, not the propagation path connecting it to the observed sympto... [Score: 9.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, tool use, reasoning, llm] Authors: Aoyang Fang, Yifan Yang, et al.

item.title: IDEA: Insensitive to Dynamics Mismatch via Effect Alignment for Sim-to-Real Transfer in Multi-Agent Control
item.description: Complex multi-agent control tasks remain challenging for traditional rule-based and model-based approaches, motivating the adoption of learning-based methods. However, learning-based methods often struggle with sim-to-real transfer because they rely on accurate dynamics modeling or system identifica... [Score: 9.0 | Flagged for: agi, agent, multi-agent, alignment, rag] Authors: Chenlong Liu, Zhuohui Zhang, et al.

item.title: From World Action Models to Embodied Brains: A Roadmap for Open-World Physical Intelligence
item.description: Artificial general intelligence ultimately requires agents that can reason and act in the physical world. Action models, vision-language-action policies, and world models have advanced this goal, while World Action Models (WAMs) are particularly promising because they connect candidate interventions... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: artificial general intelligence, world model, agent, embodied, rag] Authors: Yuanzhi Liang, Xufeng Zhan, et al.

item.title: Agentic Knowledge Tracing: A Multi-Agent LLM Architecture for Stealth Assessment of Financial Literacy in Serious Games
item.description: Assessing financial literacy during gameplay without disrupting the learning experience remains a key challenge in serious games for education. We present the Agentic BKT pipeline, a multi-agent large language model architecture for stealth assessment of financial competencies from open-ended gamepl... [Score: 8.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, agentic, llm, language model] Authors: Gabriel Santos, Rita Julia, et al.

item.title: Agentic Routing: The Harness-Native Data Flywheel
item.description: Large language model agents are increasingly executed not by a single model call, but by an execution harness that manages observation, context, control, action, state, and verification. At the same time, frontier and open models are becoming structurally specialized: a model that is strong at code ... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, tool use, reasoning, language model] Authors: Xinchen Liu, Hang Zhou, et al.

item.title: Multi-Agent LLMs Fail to Explore Each Other
item.description: Exploration is essential for reliable autonomy in multi-agent systems, yet it remains unclear whether large language model (LLM) agents can explore effectively when interacting with one another. We show that modern LLM agents fail to do so, often exhibiting myopic and polarized interaction patterns ... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, autonomy, llm, language model] Authors: Hyeong Kyu Choi, Jiatong Li, et al.

item.title: Shared Selective Persistent Memory for Agentic LLM Systems
item.description: Agentic LLM systems that generate code through multi-turn tool use face a fundamental context problem: each session starts from zero, discarding the configuration choices, domain constraints, data schemas, and tool-use patterns that made previous sessions productive. Naively persisting entire conver... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, tool use, tool-use, llm] Authors: Sanjana Pedada, Aditya Dhavala, et al.

item.title: Communication-Efficient Digital-Twin Coordination for Heterogeneous LLM Embodied Agents over Computing Power Networks
item.description: Embodied agent teams powered by heterogeneous large language models (LLMs) are being widely deployed in physical artificial intelligence such as smart factories, warehouses, and service robotics. To enable collaboration among such an agent team, efficient coordination mechanisms that operate reliabl... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, embodied, robot, robotics, llm] Authors: Nuocheng Yang, Sihua Wang, et al.

item.title: Who Broke the System? Failure Localization in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: Large language model (LLM) based multi-agent systems enable complex problem solving through coordinated reasoning and action, but their distributed structure also introduces new challenges in diagnosing system-level failures. When an execution fails, identifying which agent is responsible and at wha... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, reasoning, llm, language model] Authors: Yufei Xia, Anjun Gao, et al.

item.title: Information Limits and Attractor Dynamics in Economies of Frontier LLM Agents: A Pre-Registered Test
item.description: We report a pre-registered, two-part experiment on small economies of frontier language-model agents (Claude Opus 4.8), testing two quantitative predictions about coupled multi-agent systems: an information-theoretic capacity region for wealth growth under market coupling, and a mean-field residual-... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, alignment, llm, scaling] Authors: Cheng Qian

item.title: The Red Queen Gödel Machine: Co-Evolving Agents and Their Evaluators
item.description: Self-improving agents are state-of-the-art (SOTA) on agentic coding benchmarks and have recently been extended to general domains. However, their search methods generally assume a stationary evaluation criterion: a fixed verifier, benchmark, or labeled dataset that remains valid as the agent improve... [Score: 8.0 | Flagged for: recursive self-improvement, self-improving, agent, agentic] Authors: Alex Iacob, Andrej Jovanović, et al.

item.title: VIA: Visual Interface Agent for Robot Control
item.description: Robot manipulation is a complex task that requires visual understanding, physical reasoning, planning, and closed-loop control. General-purpose foundation models (FMs) have grown remarkably capable of some of these, especially vision and reasoning. To leverage this for generalist robot policies, cur... [Score: 7.5 | Flagged for: foundation model, agent, planning, reasoning, robot] Authors: Hengyuan Hu, Priya Sundaresan, et al.

item.title: Mosaic: Runtime-Efficient Multi-Agent Embodied Planning
item.description: LLM-based multi-agent embodied planning remains impractical due to prohibitively high execution latency. We identify failed actions as the dominant bottleneck, stemming from two core challenges: inaccurate state tracking under partial observability and inefficient coordination that produces redundan... [Score: 7.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, planning, embodied, llm] Authors: Kunjal Panchal, Saayan Mitra, et al.

item.title: WebSwarm: Recursive Multi-Agent Orchestration for Deep-and-Wide Web Search
item.description: Large language model (LLM)-based web search agents are transforming information seeking from simple factoid question answering into complex, deep-and-wide search and research-oriented tasks. A single ReAct-style agent is constrained by one long trajectory and limited context, making it difficult to ... [Score: 7.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, llm, language model, rag] Authors: Xiaoshuai Song, Liancheng Zhang, et al.

item.title: Multi-Agent Robotic Control with Onboard Vision-Language Models
item.description: Vision Language Models (VLMs) and Vision Language Action (VLA) models have shown promise in robotic control. Yet, they face significant challenges regarding explainability, generalization, and compute requirements. This paper presents a Multi-Agent System (MAS) architecture that addresses these limi... [Score: 7.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, autonomous, robot, language model] Authors: Kajetan Rachwał, Maciej Majek, et al.

item.title: Manipulation Is Task-Dependent: A Multi-Axis, Multi-Environment Evaluation of Frontier LLMs
item.description: We evaluate manipulative behavior in six frontier language models across six environments, ranging from negotiation tasks to agentic workflows, resulting in 13{,}590 individual scenarios. Manipulation rates are measured across three axes: framing (mandate honesty or permit manipulation), incentive s... [Score: 7.5 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, llm, language model, rag] Authors: Adeeb Zaman, Erik Nordby, et al.

item.title: Critique of Agent Model
item.description: What is an agent? What constitutes agency? With the rise of Large Language Model (LLM) systems marketed as ``coding agents'', ``AI co-scientists'', and other ``agentic" tools that promise to drive up productivity, and at the same time, ``existential" concerns such as AI escaping human control with d... [Score: 7.5 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, llm, language model, automation] Authors: Eric Xing, Mingkai Deng, et al.

item.title: HyperSafe: Inference-Time Safety Recovery for Fine-Tuned Language Models
item.description: Safety alignment in large language models can be fragile under fine-tuning, as even benign task adaptation may increase harmful compliance. Existing defenses mainly follow two directions: they either intervene during or after fine-tuning through retraining or weight modification, which can be costly... [Score: 7.0 | Flagged for: agi, alignment, language model, rag] Authors: Aznaur Aliev, Carlos Hinojosa, et al.

item.title: Institutional Red-Teaming: Deployment Rules, Not Just Models, Causally Shape Multi-Agent AI Safety
item.description: We introduce institutional red-teaming, an evaluation methodology for testing deployment rules in multi-agent AI: hold the agents, objectives, and task state fixed, vary only one rule, and attribute the resulting change in collective behavior to that rule. We instantiate the methodology in IABench-C... [Score: 7.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, reasoning, ai safety] Authors: Yujiao Chen

item.title: LLM-powered reasoning in agent-based modeling
item.description: Agent-based modeling (ABM) has the capability to model millions of individuals and their interactions, which is useful for policy making. However, ABMs have traditionally relied on static prior, which prevents the models from adapting to real-time changes. Our research provides a novel approach to a... [Score: 7.0 | Flagged for: agent, reasoning, llm, language model, rag] Authors: Sifat Afroj Moon, Dakotah Maguire, et al.

item.title: MAS-PromptBench: When Does Prompt Optimization Improve Multi-Agent LLM Systems?
item.description: Multi-agent systems (MAS) offer a scalable path forward for agentic AI, comprising multiple LLM-based agents, each assigned a system prompt and a position within a workflow that governs inter-agent coordination and output aggregation. System prompts thus form a critical and accessible optimization s... [Score: 7.0 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, agentic, llm] Authors: Juyang Bai, Laixi Shi

item.title: RaMem: Contextual Reinstatement for Long-term Agentic Memory
item.description: Long-term memory has become increasingly important for LLM agents that operate across extended interactions and evolving task contexts. Recent memory systems have made past experiences more persistent, compact, and retrievable, but retrieval alone does not ensure that a memory provides valid evidenc... [Score: 7.0 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, llm, retrieval, rag] Authors: Wei Yang, Bryce Kan, et al.

item.title: RAGU: A Multi-Step GraphRAG Engine with a Compact Domain-Adapted LLM
item.description: Graph retrieval-augmented generation (GraphRAG) enhances large language models with structured knowledge, yet existing systems construct knowledge graphs in a single extraction pass, producing noisy entities and brittle retrieval. RAGU, an open-source modular GraphRAG engine, addresses this by separ... [Score: 6.5 | Flagged for: reasoning, llm, language model, retrieval, rag] Authors: Mikhail Komarov, Ivan Bondarenko, et al.

item.title: The Ebb and Flow of Multimodal Focus: Scheduling Visual Relay Windows for Grounded VLM Reasoning
item.description: Vision-language models increasingly succeed on multimodal reasoning benchmarks, yet their visual evidence often becomes unstable once it enters the language stack, weakening evidence-grounded reasoning. To understand this fragility, we examine the internal dynamics of VLMs through a mechanistic lens... [Score: 6.5 | Flagged for: agi, reasoning, language model, rag] Authors: Wencheng Ye, Yi Bin, et al.

item.title: ToFu: A White-Box, Token-Efficient Agent Harness for Researchers
item.description: Agentic coding tools present new opportunities to transform research workflows. The performance of agent systems built depends on both large language models (LLMs) and the harness around LLMs, which is the orchestration code that determines an agent's behavior. We present ToFu, an agentic harness fo... [Score: 6.5 | Flagged for: agent, agentic, llm, language model] Authors: Junhao Ruan, Yuan Ge, et al.

item.title: Towards Predictive, Aligned, and Scalable Robot Learning
item.description: Learning, at its core, extends beyond memorization to the ability to reason and solve novel problems by navigating a space of possibilities. We introduce Lumo-2, a latent world-action model that generates actions by reasoning over world dynamics in latent space. The learned latent world dynamics cap... [Score: 6.5 | Flagged for: world model, reasoning, alignment, robot] Authors: Peijun Tang, Shangjin Xie, et al.

item.title: Multi-Agent Firewall Architecture for Privacy Protection of Sensitive Data in Interactions with Language Models
item.description: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have become essential productivity tools, their integration into workflows without adequate safeguards creates significant risks. This paper proposes an open-source, privacy-focused, user-facing firewall designed to secure both web-based and programmatic LLM intera... [Score: 6.5 | Flagged for: agent, multi-agent, llm, language model] Authors: Hugo García Cuesta, Pablo Mateo Torrejón, et al.
